dev.changes_summary: Consolidated 5 independent /allow grant-read implementations into hooks/lib/allowlist.py. Added _match_loaded_grant (pure match, no I/O), _load_and_match (blocking LOCK_EX wrapper), read_grant_for_git_command (substr_only literal policy), match_grant_for_bash_command (NB-flock+retry, compound split, SIGALRM), and consume_grant_for_posttool (atomic match+unlink, sole tool-execution consumption site). Updated all 4 callers to delegate to lib. All IS_SUBAGENT firewalls preserved in callers. Force-push ordering invariant preserved.

tasks_completed.changes: Added MatchResult NamedTuple; _match_loaded_grant (pure match, no I/O, literal_policy parameter); _load_and_match (blocking LOCK_EX wrapper calling _match_loaded_grant); updated read_grant to delegate to _load_and_match; added read_grant_for_git_command (substr_only semantics); match_grant_for_bash_command (NB-flock+3x100ms retry, compound split on &&||;|, SIGALRM timeout, calls _match_loaded_grant directly); consume_grant_for_posttool (atomic match+unlink under single LOCK_EX, full 4-branch dispatch, sole os.unlink site)
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: incremental development left 4 independent open/flock/JSON-load/match implementations (commit aaf3c44 extracted lib but migration was partial)

tasks_completed.changes: Removed 25-line independent open+flock+JSON+match block; added sys.path.insert + from lib.allowlist import read_grant_for_git_command; replaced body with 3-line IS_SUBAGENT check + _get_session_id + read_grant_for_git_command call
tasks_completed.rationale: IS_SUBAGENT check (data.get('agent_id')) preserved in caller before lib call; force-push ordering invariant maintained (_push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 554 precedes _check_git_allowlist at line 561 in _evaluate_push)

tasks_completed.description: M5: replaced pretool-bash-safety.sh heredoc (lines 163-323) with shim
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced 160-line Python heredoc with 16-line shim. Set HOOKS_DIR via BASH_SOURCE in bash context before heredoc. Shim uses sys.path.insert(0, os.environ['HOOKS_DIR']), imports match_grant_for_bash_command from lib.allowlist, calls it, emits CONSUMED protocol on MatchResult. IS_SUBAGENT check remains in bash wrapper (line 145)
tasks_completed.rationale: BASH_SOURCE not exported to Python (subprocess); __file__ resolves to <stdin> in heredoc mode; HOOKS_DIR set in bash context is the correct pattern per spec Technical Hints

tasks_completed.changes: Removed independent 52-line grant-read+match+unlink block (lines 53-104); removed unused _split_bash_command function; added from lib.allowlist import consume_grant_for_posttool; replaced block with 5-line call consume_grant_for_posttool(session_id, tool_name, command); removed unused imports (fcntl, re); sys.exit(0) preserved
tasks_completed.rationale: IS_SUBAGENT check (is_subagent_context) and session_id resolution remain in posttool caller; consume_grant_for_posttool is now the sole tool-execution unlink site

tasks_completed.description: M6: replaced pretool-write-guard.sh inline Python block (lines 32-54)
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced 20-line inline python3 -c block with heredoc subprocess that sets HOOKS_DIR from BASH_SOURCE and calls read_grant('Write', sid). IS_SUBAGENT check (_WG_IS_SUB) and /do flag check (_WG_DO_FLAG) remain before the lib call
tasks_completed.rationale: Fixed string 'Write' matches read_grant's exact_or_substr semantics exactly — no new library function needed

tasks_completed.changes: 25 unit tests covering: _match_loaded_grant (pure match semantics), _load_and_match (file I/O integration), read_grant, read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command (compound split, NB-flock), consume_grant_for_posttool (AC5/AC8: exact-only non-Bash prevents TodoWrite consume, Bash compound command consumed correctly, Bash fallback to tool_name), IS_SUBAGENT firewall via _check_git_allowlist import test
tasks_completed.rationale: AC8 requires: (a) subagent payload returns False for git guard; (b) literal Write grant does not consume TodoWrite; (c) Bash compound command is consumed

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: aaf3c44 — refactor(hooks): extract shared lib/subagent.py and lib/allowlist.py; fix git-privilege-guard read-only
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Task aaf3c44 extracted lib/allowlist.py from pretool-orchestrator-gate.py but migration was partial — git guard renamed function without converting to library, bash heredoc and posttool were explicitly left as-is
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: All 4 independent grant-file open/flock/JSON-load/match implementations now delegate to lib/allowlist.py; format/semantics change now requires only one edit location

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Net change is +25 lines across 5 files after removing ~284 duplicate lines and adding ~309 library lines. Library functions require thorough docstrings and type annotations per contract (AC1, AC5 signature requirements). No extra scope added beyond BA spec.

dev.qa_notes: Run hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py (25 tests, all pass). Verify: (1) no os.unlink in posttool, (2) no grant-file open in git guard, (3) no fcntl.flock/json.load/claude-bash-allowlist- in allowlist heredoc (lines 166-183), (4) IS_SUBAGENT checks present before lib calls in all callers, (5) force-push block (line 554) precedes _check_git_allowlist (line 561) in _evaluate_push. Note: grep for fcntl.flock in bash hook will find results in daemon-restart heredoc (lines ~307-384) — this is expected and out-of-scope for AC7.

self_verification.notes: All 10 verification checks from spec passed: (1) allowlist.py syntax OK, (2) pretool-git-privilege-guard.py syntax OK, (3) posttool-allowlist-consume.py syntax OK, (4) pretool-bash-safety.sh bash -n OK, (5) pretool-write-guard.sh bash -n OK, (6) no os.unlink in posttool, (7) no claude-bash-allowlist in git guard, (8) no fcntl.flock in allowlist heredoc (daemon-restart heredoc has it, out-of-scope), (9) IS_SUBAGENT check present in bash hook, (10) _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 554 precedes _check_git_allowlist at line 561 in _evaluate_push. 25/25 unit tests pass.

recommendations: Consider updating the comment at pretool-bash-safety.sh line 158-160 to reflect the new shim architecture (no longer describes V1/V2/V3 embedded in heredoc)

The _split_bash_command function was removed from posttool — if the docstring reference 'Mirrors pretool-bash-safety.sh split_subcommands (lines 227-230)' was relied on elsewhere, note those line numbers are now different
